Consensus estimates call for $1.76 EPS on $78.8B revenue, but investors will focus on forward guidance and the company's role in AI infrastructure. The options market is pricing in a ~5.5% post-earnings move, with bullish positioning dominating as call demand significantly outpaces put demand.

title: Why Marvell Technology Stock Is Moving On Up

title: The AI Stock That Could Quietly Double Your Retirement Savings Over the Next Decade
summary: Nvidia is positioned as a generational investment opportunity that could double retirement savings over the next decade. The chipmaker benefits from exponential computing demand driven by agentic AI, which requires 1,000 times more tokens per task than standard chatbot queries. With a $5.3 trillion market cap, 73% year-over-year revenue growth, and dominant market share in AI processors, Nvidia maintains substantial pricing power despite competition. The company's continued R&D investments in next-generation chips like Vera Rubin position it to remain the dominant supplier of AI infrastructure as tech giants accelerate capital expenditures.

title: OpenAI And Google Deepen Singapore AI Investment Push

These strategic investments position Singapore as the regional AI hub for Southeast Asia, with implications for cloud infrastructure demand and GPU compute needs. The moves reflect broader geopolitical shifts as U.S. AI companies establish footholds in Asia-Pacific amid U.S.-China competition.

title: Nvidia's Earnings Are Hours Away. Here Are 3 Things to Watch.
summary: Nvidia reports fiscal Q1 earnings with three key items to watch: management's guidance for Q2 (critical indicator of AI demand momentum), progress on the Vera Rubin next-generation chip platform, and any updates on China market opportunities following recent export restriction easing. The stock trades at a P/E of 45, up 19% year-to-date, with hyperscaler capex spending continuing to accelerate.

title: Bill Ackman's Alphabet Sale Has Cost $641 Million — Will His New Mag 7 Pick Save The Trade?
